title: Including a General Dataset Mix
concept: A practical fix: mix a small portion of general pretraining data (e.g. 10%) into your domain-specific fine-tuning dataset to maintain general capabilities.

exercises.prompt: What is a common risk when you train an AI model ONLY train an AI on highly specialized data?

options.text: It might forget how to do basic, everyday tasks.
It will instantly become smarter at everything.
Its file size will automatically shrink to zero.

exercises.explain: When models focus solely on one new topic, they tend to overwrite and lose the general knowledge they learned earlier.

cards.title: The Forgetful Expert
cards.body: When you teach an AI to become a super-focused expert, like a medical doctor, it can easily 'forget' how to do basic things—like chat politely or write a simple email. This is called catastrophic forgetting. How do we keep our AI smart at the basics while it learns a specialty?

cards.title: What is a Dataset Mix?
cards.body: To prevent an AI from losing its general skills during specialized training, we use a 'general dataset mix'. This means we add a small amount (like 10%) of general text—like books or websites—directly into our specialized training data.

cards.title: The Specialized Chef
cards.body: Imagine you are training to be a specialized pastry chef, but you completely stop cooking normal meals. Soon, you might forget how to boil an egg! By keeping just 10% of your weekly cooking dedicated to basic everyday recipes, you keep your general cooking skills sharp while still becoming a master of pastries.

cards.title: A Legal AI Mix
cards.body: If you have 9,000 documents of specialized legal contracts, you can mix in 1,000 documents of general news articles and Wikipedia pages. This 10% mix ensures your legal AI doesn't forget how to write everyday emails or answer simple general-knowledge questions.

cards.title: The 100% Expert Myth
cards.body: Many people think that to make a model an expert, its training data must be 100% specialized. In reality, pure specialized training often ruins the model's conversational flow. Adding a small splash of general data actually makes the expert model work much better!

cards.title: Compare the Training Styles
cards.body: See how adding a small mix of general data changes what the AI remembers.

left.text: Answers technical questions perfectly, but fails to write a polite 'hello' or understand basic metaphors.

right.text: Answers technical questions perfectly AND writes friendly, natural emails using its general skills.

exercises.prompt: How much general pretraining data is typically mixed into specialized training to maintain general skills?

options.text: A small mix, like 10% general data.
99% general data and only 1% specialized data.

exercises.explain: A small portion (often around 10%) of general data is usually enough to preserve general conversational skills without distracting the AI from its main specialized goal.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Specialized AIs never need to answer general questions, so we should completely remove general data.
exercises.explain: Even specialized AIs need general knowledge to communicate naturally and understand basic context.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe what happens when a model loses its basic skills.
exercises.sentence: Mixing in general pretraining data helps prevent catastrophic ________.
exercises.explain: Catastrophic forgetting happens when an AI overwrites its general knowledge to learn new, specialized skills. Mixing in general data prevents this.

exercises.prompt: Identify the AI model that uses a dataset mix to keep its everyday conversation skills while learning medicine.

options.text: Model A: Trained on 100% medical medical records.
Model B: Trained on 90% medical records and 10% general websites.
Model C: Trained on 100% general websites only.

exercises.explain: Only AI Model B has the correct mix of general data to keep its general skills sharp, while Model A is at risk of catastrophic forgetting.

exercises.prompt: You are deploying a medical AI assistant. Doctors love its medical knowledge, but patients are confused because the AI has forgotten how to write friendly, everyday greetings. How would you fix this problem during its training?

options.text: By training exclusively on specialized data so it forgets all general knowledge.
By mixing a small percentage of general web data into the specialized training data.
By deleting the model's original pretraining history and starting over.
By only training the model on weekends to let it rest.

exercises.explain: Adding a small portion (around 10%) of general pretraining data acts as a memory anchor, keeping the AI's general language skills sharp while it masters a new specialized domain.
